clinical_notes: Patient has been on Metformin 1000mg and Glipizide for 8 months with inadequate glycemic control. HbA1c remains at 9.2%. No history of pancreatitis. Renal function normal (eGFR 85). Requesting approval for Ozempic (semaglutide) injection.
Patient reports mild tension headaches 1-2 times per month, relieved by rest. No red flag symptoms. No prior treatment attempted.

Patient has failed conservative management including NSAIDs, physical therapy (12 weeks), and corticosteroid injections (2 rounds) over the past year. X-ray confirms bone-on-bone arthritis. Significant functional impairment affecting ADLs.
Patient has mild seasonal allergy symptoms during spring. Has not tried any over-the-counter antihistamines yet.

Patient has failed adequate trials of 3 different SSRIs/SNRIs (sertraline, venlafaxine, bupropion) over 18 months, each at therapeutic doses for 8+ weeks. PHQ-9 score remains 19 (severe). Referred by psychiatrist for esketamine treatment.
Patient has advanced Parkinson disease with inadequate motor control despite optimised medication. Tremor and rigidity significantly impair ADLs. Neurologist recommends DBS evaluation.
